items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: WA Food Minister Jackie Jarvis warns of 'challenging times ahead' for groceries
items.summary: WA Agriculture and Food Minister Jackie Jarvis says natural disasters as well as fuel and fertiliser availability and cost increases are contributing to real concerns around food supply in WA.

items.title: Coroner recommends overhaul of prescription drug use inside Corella Place after 'preventable death'
items.summary: A coronial inquest has found that a supervised facility for sex offenders allowed a resident to have control over prescribed medications, which contributed to his death.

items.title: Cage fighter was 'mucking around' when backyard spar near Mount Druitt turned deadly, jury hears
items.summary: Jurors have heard that the accused's girlfriend suggested he call an ambulance after the sparring session, but he told her to "hurry up and just get here".

items.title: Darwin middle school teacher who broke female student's teeth found guilty of causing serious harm
items.summary: A former Darwin Middle School teacher who struck a female student in the face, breaking her teeth, after she demanded her confiscated phone back has been found guilty by a jury of unlawfully causing serious harm.

items.title: Taylor's migration plan doesn't make much sense. That's not the point
items.summary: More than a day after Angus Taylor unveiled the first plank of the Coalition's long-awaited migration policy and many media interviews later, it's still unclear what the opposition is actually proposing to change.

items.title: Anthony Albanese meets with Sultan of Brunei to shore up fertiliser and fuel supplies
items.summary: Brunei may be able to help Australia weather the economic chaos caused by the Iran war by sending additional supplies of fertiliser, the prime minister says.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz blockade could turn into global agrifood crisis
items.summary: The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations says ships in the Strait of Hormuz need to start moving again to avoid a global agrifood crisis. Here's why.

items.title: Farmers selling cattle in record numbers as feed becomes scarce and El Niño forecast
items.summary: More than 500,000 head of cattle have already been sold across New South Wales this year, with Scone seeing its biggest sale in 20 years.

items.title: Youths pleading guilty to crimes they may not have committed under new bail laws, NSW inquiry hears
items.summary: Several lawyers have told a state parliament inquiry into the youth justice system that bail laws are encouraging children to plead guilty to crimes they may not have committed.

items.title: Trucking body calls for immediate action from federal government to stem business closures
items.summary: Trucking industry peak group NatRoad calls on the federal government to urgently implement the $1 billion Economic Resilience Program it promised two weeks ago.

items.title: Power confirms it will appeal after AFL Tribunal releases reasons for Zak Butters guilty finding
items.summary: The AFL Tribunal releases its reasons for finding Zak Butters guilty of abusive and insulting language to umpire Nick Foot, saying it was "satisfied to the requisite standard" that he had made the remarks and that it was "implausible" that the umpire would have made it up.

items.title: PNG hotel owner looks to sue Australian government for $11m in unpaid refugee accommodation costs
items.summary: A hotel in Papua New Guinea is attempting to sue the Australian government over accommodation costs for refugees previously detained on Manus Island.

items.title: Duke of Sussex mingles with attendees at Invictus event after sombre commemorations in Canberra
items.summary: The Duke of Sussex flew solo to the nation's capital and addressed an Invictus Australia event at the Australian War Memorial.

items.title: Emerson calls for CIT CEO to stand down amid claims newly released documents show she 'misled' the board
items.summary: An independent MLA is calling for the head of CIT to stand down, claiming statutory declarations show she "clearly misled" the board prior to her appointment, despite knowing about misconduct findings.

items.title: Petrol drive-offs prompt arrest after alleged theft of $12,000 worth of fuel
items.summary: A 36-year-old man has been charged with theft amid an investigation into as many as 20 petrol drive-offs, which police say involved an SUV that contained a tank used to carry stolen diesel.

items.title: Iran war's exposure of Australia's fertiliser deficit could inspire manufacturing comeback
items.summary: Projects are in the works to expand Australia's domestic fertiliser supply as prices rise by 60 per cent.

items.title: Agriculture expert says fertiliser shortage has lesser domestic impact on food supply
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Kanye West postpones show in France until further notice
items.summary: US rapper Kanye West has postponed his upcoming concert in the French city of Marseille, following reports that the country's interior minister was seeking to block the performance.

items.title: Landmark plea as teen admits creating or altering sexually explicit deepfakes
items.summary: An Adelaide teenager who was the first person charged under new nationwide laws aimed at tackling adult deepfake pornography has pleaded guilty to two counts of creating or altering sexual material without consent.

items.title: Tropical cyclone Sinlaku causes significant damage in Northern Mariana Islands and Guam
items.summary: A super typhoon with ferocious winds and heavy rains made landfall in the Commonwealth of Northern Mariana Islands (CNMI) overnight, destroying homes and infrastructure.

items.title: Earthquake captured on camera in bird nesting box in Orange
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Wright Prospecting awarded royalties from Hope Downs mine after legal battle with Gina Rinehart
items.summary: Gina Rinehart is dealt a blow, with a court finding the family of her father's former business partner has the right to potentially hundreds of millions of dollars in royalties from lucrative Pilbara mining assets.

items.title: Property developer allegedly criticised mayor's age and intellect in harassment case
items.summary: A Southern Highlands developer facing harassment allegations says legal action is politically motivated, while police allege a pattern of emails and conduct targeting senior council officials.

items.title: Frenchman scoops up 1-million-euro Picasso painting in Paris raffle
items.summary: A man from Paris has won a Picasso painting worth 1 million euros ($1.6 million) with a 100-euro raffle ticket.

items.title: Victorian independent candidates win High Court battle over cap on political donations
items.summary: Victoria's political donation and disclosure laws have been scrapped by the High Court just seven months out from the state's upcoming election.

items.title: Police arrest 18yo man over armed home invasion on Gold Coast
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: How white should your teeth actually be?
items.summary: Experts say there are many factors that influence how white a tooth appears, and it's not just about whitening or what we’re eating.

items.title: Newmont's Cadia gold mine suspends underground operations after earthquake in Orange
items.summary: All underground workers at Newmont's Cadia gold mine have been brought to the surface as inspections are carried out following an earthquake.

items.title: King Charles to woo Trump and restore 'closest of friendships' on US visit
items.summary: King Charles will have tea and a private meeting with US President Donald Trump during his US state visit later this month, with trips to Washington, New York and Virginia.

items.title: Super Rugby Pacific's Moana Pasifika say 2026 season will be team's last due to finances and other challenges
items.summary: Citing financial, operational, and strategic barriers that have made continuing unviable, battling Super Rugby Pacific outfit Moana Pasifika reveal they will disband at season's end.

items.title: Zak Butters verbal abuse verdict raises questions about AFL's 'conflict of interest' with gambling
items.summary: The reporting of Port Adelaide player Zak Butters for umpire abuse sparks debate about whether officials should be involved with betting and if better audio recordings should be available from games.

items.title: Pub revival underway in historic outback Queensland town of Barcaldine
items.summary: A revival is brewing in Barcaldine, with the renovation and reopening of two pubs the talk of the town.

items.title: New footage captures moment Artemis II crew welcomed home
items.summary: New footage shows the moment a recovery crew opens the hatch of Artemis II's Orion capsule and welcomes its crew members home after their mission around the Moon.

items.title: Lance Collard's hearing and monster sanction leaves no winners, as queer community further bruised
items.summary: Lance Collard is handed a monster suspension after being found guilty of using a homophobic slur in the first AFL disciplinary hearing.

items.title: NSW pharmacists will soon be able to prescribe the contraceptive pill
items.summary: Patients will no longer have to visit the doctor to be dispensed the contraceptive pill, under a new pharmacy prescription scheme.

items.title: PSG beats Liverpool, Atlético Madrid downs Barcelona to reach Champions League semifinals
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain and Atlético Madrid advance to the last four of the Champions League, as Liverpool's rough season continues with a key injury that deals a blow to France's World Cup chances.

items.title: Lebanon, Israel agree to direct negotiations after Washington talks
items.summary: Israel and Lebanon agree to direct negotiations following talks in Washington hailed by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio as a "historic opportunity" for peace.

items.title: Family hopes epilepsy Senate inquiry will shine light on funding gaps
items.summary: Four-year-old Leo has a rare form of epilepsy, and his family has turned to creative ways to fund help for him. An advocacy group says others are in the same boat, and it hopes a new inquiry will help.

items.title: After backlash over World Cup ticket prices, FIFA provides more expensive options
items.summary: After a backlash over ticket prices, FIFA puts up a new option for fans — and it is even more expensive.

items.title: Tributes flow for 'extraordinary' Robe sculptor and horsewoman Patricia Moseley
items.summary: Across dusty outback tracks to the promenades of Adelaide, sculptor Pat Moseley turned limestone and bronze into the faces of legends.

items.title: ASX follows Wall Street higher, oil prices sink on hope of further US-Iran peace talks — as it happened
items.summary: The Australian share market has risen in morning trade as global investors bet on the US-Iran war being over within months, despite fuel shortages, higher inflation and a possible economic slowdown.

items.title: Scientists push to have platypus listed as 'vulnerable' on conservation register
items.summary: A lack of hard data on platypus numbers holds back a Queensland-led effort to list the iconic monotreme as "vulnerable" on the national Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation register.

items.title: Platypus spotted outside hotel in Marian, Queensland
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Chalmers says budget will not be as planned but is still 'ambitious' — as it happened
items.summary: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says the budget he will hand down next month is not what he initially planned, due to the Iran war.

items.title: Your brain explained: Why it's getting harder to pay attention
items.summary: If it feels harder to concentrate than it used to, it's not just you. Scientists say your brain is reacting exactly as it was designed to — but in a very different world.

items.title: NT Aboriginal affairs framework released as territory goes backwards on Closing the Gap targets
items.summary: The Northern Territory government has released its Aboriginal Affairs framework as the territory goes backwards on several Closing the Gap targets.

items.title: Australia's Cameron Green' poor IPL season continues in Kolkata Knight Riders' loss to Chennai Super Kings
items.summary: Cameron Green has suffered another challenging outing in the Indian Premier League as he struggles to live up to his tag as the most expensive overseas player.

items.title: Thousands of NSW students embrace AI to learn but environmental, creativity concerns remain
items.summary: Thousands of students are using AI to transform the way they learn, but many remain concerned about what the technology means for the environment, jobs and creativity.

items.title: Man,18, charged with attempted murder after early morning Gold Coast home invasion
items.summary: Police allege the man forced his way into a Carrara home about 4am yesterday and stabbed two residents. He is expected to appear in court today.

items.title: Victorian opposition announces bail, sentencing policy including 'one strike' bail rule
items.summary: The Victorian opposition has pledged to expand the government's "adult time for violent crime" laws and introduce a "one-strike" bail rule that would see re-offenders automatically denied bail.

items.title: Flood watches issued as former Cyclone Maila brings days of heavy rain
items.summary: Minor flood watches have been issued for several Far North Queensland catchments as former Tropical Cyclone Maila delivers sustained heavy rainfall, with forecasters warning transport routes could be disrupted.

items.title: Israel-Lebanon peace talks begin but Hezbollah won't be held to any deal. What you need to know on Iran today
items.summary: One week into the ceasefire, two sets of peace talks have taken place but the only commitment is to talk more.

items.title: Gosford Hospital leading Australia with new lifesaving stroke prevention surgery from US
items.summary: A NSW hospital becomes the first in Australia to offer a new stroke-prevention treatment that has been available in the United States for a decade.

items.title: El Niño in 2026 could be one of the strongest on record — what does it mean for Australia's weather?
items.summary: Australia's weather is expected to shift rapidly, thanks to the probable emergence of an El Niño, meaning drought, extreme heat and bushfires could be on the way.

items.title: Ombudsman report into interaction between Queensland Health and boys with disabilities makes 13 recommendations
items.summary: A 48-page report has been released on the interactions between health care workers and two siblings who were discovered living in squalor in May 2020.

items.title: The outback jobs disappearing as tourists can no longer afford fuel to travel
items.summary: From roadhouses to stations, outback workers are losing their jobs as tourists stay away. Skyrocketing fuel prices, as well as concerns around the availability of fuel in remote locations, are causing a 76 per cent loss of revenue for tourist-dependent businesses.

items.title: Horsham Town Hall postpones performances after hidden cameras found in change rooms
items.summary: An investigation into hidden cameras in change rooms has forced the Horsham Town Hall to postpone events in western Victoria.

items.title: Undertone's director taps into our fear of things that go bump in the night
items.summary: Filmed in director Ian Tuason's childhood home and based on his own experiences caring for his dying parents, Undertone has become a sleeper hit.

items.title: Should you turn off your laptop while commuting?
items.summary: While modern laptops are good at saving battery life and keeping cool, extended periods in sleep mode could still be putting a strain on them over time.

items.title: Travel quiz, April 15: We're going on a trip — don't forget your toothbrush
items.summary: What are the pyramids made out of? And which Caribbean island doesn't appear in the Beach Boys song Kokomo?

items.title: Trump lashes out at European ally Meloni amid tensions over pope and NATO
items.summary: The US president says Giorgia Meloni "does not care" about Iran getting a nuclear weapon, after the Italian PM defended the pope and cancelled a military deal with Israel.

items.title: Trump says war is 'very close to over' as mediators push ceasefire extension
items.summary: US President Donald Trump says he views the Iran war as "very close to over" in his latest comments amid a fragile ceasefire between the two countries.

items.title: Mark Carney secures majority government in Canada as Liberals win three by-elections
items.summary: The Canadian prime minister said voters had "placed their trust" in his government and its "clear understanding of what this moment demands".
